title: Tom VS Code Extension Configuration
description: User configuration for the Tom VS Code extension. Covers anthropic, compaction, memory, and trail subsystems (anthropic_sdk_integration.md §14).

sendToChatTarget.description: Which chat transport 'Send to Chat' routes to. 'anthropic' (default) handles the prompt as if entered in the Anthropic panel (same profile + tools, answer from the Anthropic transport, shown in the live trail, blocks while executing). 'copilot' routes to GitHub Copilot Chat. Also drives tools.getJsonVce: the scripting-API tools JSON is empty when set to 'copilot'.

mcpServer.description: Standalone MCP server (mcp_server_implementation_plan.md §6/§7). A separate, independently-configured surface from the scripting-API / CLI bridge: its own tool picker (not the chat profile), its own inbound API key, and its own lifecycle. Transport is always Streamable HTTP bound to 'host'. On start the server probes upward from 'basePort' to the first free port — every window runs its own server — so the actually-bound port is runtime state, surfaced in the UI, never written back here.

enabled.description: Master on/off switch for the MCP server.

autoStart.description: Start the server on extension activation.

host.description: Bind address. Default 0.0.0.0 (reachable over the VPN).

basePort.description: First port to try. The server probes upward from here to the first free port; the actually-bound port is runtime state, not stored. Default 19920 (clear of the CLI server's 19900).

apiKeyEnv.description: NAME of the env var holding the expected inbound bearer token (never the secret). Empty = no auth configured = read-only floor applies.

allowWriteWithoutAuth.description: When true, unauthenticated clients also get the configured write tools. Default false = unauthenticated access is read-only.

toolsEnabled.description: true = expose all tools; false = use enabledTools. Default true.

enabledTools.description: Independent allow-list (own picker, NOT the chat profile). Honored when toolsEnabled is false.

questRefresh.description: Quest Refresh (quest_refresh_implementation_plan.md). An automatic 'refresh prompt' that fires every N prompts to run maintenance work off the recent trail (update overview, prune todos, refresh quest-notes). These settings are GLOBAL (shared across quests); the per-quest activation flag + the prompt counter live in _ai/quests/{questId}/quest-refresh.{hostname}.{questId}.yaml (QuestRefreshStore, host-specific so the shared _ai clone doesn't collide state across machines), not here. Copilot is wired into the UI but its auto-trigger is deferred.

apiKeyEnvVar.description: Environment variable name from which the Anthropic API key is read at runtime. No key material is stored in this file.

configurations.description: Named Anthropic configurations selectable in the ANTHROPIC panel section.

profiles.description: System-prompt profiles. A profile binds a system prompt to a configuration.

transportRetry.description: Agent SDK transport retry (agent-sdk-transport.ts). Retries a failed SDK stream, resuming the session with a continuation prompt or repeating on a fresh session when the session id is missing/unknown.

maxAttempts.description: Total attempts including the first. 1 disables retry.

templateId.description: Selected continuation-prompt template id (from templates). Empty = built-in default.

templates.description: Continuation-prompt templates (transportRetry category). Bodies typically reference ${errorText} and ${userMessage}.

isDefault.description: Marks the template that "use default" (empty templateId) resolves to. At most one.

interactiveQuestionsTemplates.description: Fallback templates for the Agent SDK built-in AskUserQuestion tool (agent-sdk-questions.ts). Used as the tool result when a profile does not allow interactive questions (or the user dismisses the picker), telling the agent to proceed autonomously. Bodies may reference ${questions} (a digest of the skipped questions). Selected per-profile via interactiveQuestionsTemplateId.

disabled.description: Global kill switch. When true, the background compaction + memory extraction pass after every Anthropic turn is skipped entirely (both direct and Agent SDK transports). Use when you want the SDK to own conversation state by itself, or when the extra API call per turn isn't worth it.

llmProvider.description: Which provider runs the compaction LLM.

llmConfigId.description: Configuration id within the selected provider.

compactionTemplateId.description: Active 'compaction' template id (managed via Global Template Editor).

memoryExtractionTemplateId.description: Active 'memoryExtraction' template id (managed via Global Template Editor).

enabledTools.description: Restricted tool set for the compaction pass (Local LLM only).

compactionMaxRounds.description: Maximum tool-call rounds during a single compaction pass.

historyMaxChars.description: Maximum character size of the history (compacted summary + raw turns) injected into the compaction and memory-extraction prompts. Exposed as ${historyMaxChars} to the compaction template so the LLM targets output within this size.

memoryMaxChars.description: Maximum character size of the existing memory file content injected into the memory-extraction prompt. Older entries beyond this cap are dropped before the LLM sees them.

toolTrailMaxResultChars.description: Per tool-result truncation when injected back into the model's prompt. Applies to both Local LLM and Anthropic tool loops. Full bodies are persisted via TrailService and recoverable through `tomAi_readPastToolResult({key})`. Per-configuration overrides take precedence.

toolTrailKeepRounds.description: Number of most-recent tool-result blocks kept in-message (truncated to toolTrailMaxResultChars each). Older tool_result blocks are replaced with a stub naming the key (e.g. `[Past tool call t14 — tomAi_readFile(...) — 61823 chars. Use tomAi_readPastToolResult({key:'t14'}) to retrieve.]`). Per-configuration overrides take precedence.

rawTurnsKept.description: Number of most-recent user/assistant turn pairs (2 messages each) kept verbatim in the prompt. Overflow is folded into the incremental compacted summary via the active compaction template. Together with maxHistoryTokens — turns is primary, tokens is the safety cap. Per-configuration overrides take precedence.

runEveryNRounds.description: Round-based trigger for compaction + memory extraction. A round = one completed user→assistant exchange. The uncompacted-rounds accumulator (compaction_rounds.json, sibling of history.json, NOT versioned) grows by one round per turn; when it reaches this threshold compaction fires, folds the oldest (length - rawTurnsKept) rounds into the running summary, and shrinks back to rawTurnsKept. Memory extraction runs in the same pass. Also the chunk size when rebuilding history from trail files. Single knob; both subsystems share the cadence so the prompt prefix stays cache-stable for runEveryNRounds−1 turns at a time.

backgroundExtractionEnabled.description: Enable implicit llm_extract memory writes after each turn.

enabled.description: Master switch for the memory subsystem.

path.description: Workspace-relative root directory for memory files.

maxInjectedTokens.description: Token cap for the ${memory} / ${memory-shared} / ${memory-quest} placeholders. Memory is no longer auto-injected into the system prompt — add the placeholder to your profile's system prompt or user-message template to opt in.

maxEntries.description: Max entries in consolidated summary trail files (prompts.md / answers.md) before oldest are trimmed.

maxRawFiles.description: Max total raw trail files (prompt, answer, payload, tool_request, tool_answer combined) per quest directory. Oldest files are deleted when the limit is exceeded.

questRefreshPanel.description: Per-panel Quest Refresh configuration. Global (shared across quests).

promptInterval.description: Number of prompts between refreshes. 0 (default) = never auto-refresh.

refreshPrompt.description: The refresh prompt text dispatched through the panel's transport when the interval elapses.

memoryToolsEnabled.description: Expose memory read/write tools to the model.

rawTurnsKept.description: Per-configuration override for compaction.rawTurnsKept (raw turn pairs kept inline).

promptCachingEnabled.description: Send the system prompt as cache_control blocks for prompt caching (anthropic_sdk_integration.md §16).

transport.description: Backend selector (anthropic_sdk_integration.md §18, multi_transport_prompt_queue_revised.md §4.2). 'direct' uses @anthropic-ai/sdk with apiKeyEnvVar. 'agentSdk' routes through @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk, inheriting auth from the host Claude Code install and delegating tool-use loop, prompt caching, and compaction to the SDK. 'vscodeLm' routes through vscode.lm.selectChatModels/sendRequest — model is pinned at configure-time via the vscodeLm options below.

vscodeLm.description: VS Code LM API options; applies when transport === 'vscodeLm'. Model identity is pinned at configure-time.

modelId.description: Exact model id within the (vendor, family) pair. Picked at configure-time from vscode.lm.selectChatModels() results.

agentSdk.description: Agent SDK specific options; ignored when transport !== 'agentSdk'.

permissionMode.description: SDK permission mode. 'default' prompts for dangerous ops via canUseTool; 'acceptEdits' auto-accepts file edits; 'plan' never executes tools; 'bypassPermissions' disables prompts entirely.

settingSources.description: Which filesystem settings layers the SDK should load. Empty array = isolation mode (no filesystem settings).

maxTurns.description: Turn cap for the agent loop. When omitted, the configuration's maxRounds is used.

compactionOverride.description: Per-configuration override for the global 'Disable compaction & memory extraction' checkbox. 'default' uses the global flag; 'on' forces compaction ON for this configuration; 'off' forces compaction OFF.

configurationId.description: Default configuration id for this profile.

toolsEnabled.description: If true (default), expose every tool in ALL_SHARED_TOOLS. Uncheck to pick a profile-specific subset via enabledTools below.

enabledTools.description: Profile-level tool subset. Only used when toolsEnabled is false.

thinkingEnabled.description: Enable Claude extended thinking. Passes `thinking: { type: 'enabled', budget_tokens }` on the direct SDK; forwarded to the Agent SDK when set.

thinkingBudgetTokens.description: Token budget for extended thinking. Ignored if thinkingEnabled is false.

promptCachingEnabled.description: Prompt caching for this profile. Overrides configuration.promptCachingEnabled. Default on at the profile level to reduce latency / cost.

toolApprovalMode.description: Approval gate for write tool calls. 'always' shows the approval bar before every write tool (the user can elevate a single approval to the full session via the 'Allow All (session)' button at the bar). 'never' skips the gate entirely (dangerous); on the Agent SDK transport it also forces permissionMode=bypassPermissions.

useBuiltInTools.description: Agent SDK transport only: expose Claude Code's built-in tool preset (Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash, WebFetch, etc.) and automatically suppress extension tools that duplicate them. No effect on the direct Anthropic SDK.

autoInjectMemory.description: When true, append a `## Memory\n\n${memory}` section to the resolved system prompt automatically. When false (default), memory is only injected when the profile explicitly references `${memory}` / `${memory-shared}` / `${memory-quest}` in its System Prompt, User Prompt Wrapper, or user-message template. Note: recursive expansion may still inline memory through file-injection placeholders like `${quest-description}` if those files contain memory tokens — audit those files separately.

allowInteractiveQuestions.description: Agent SDK transport only: when the agent calls the built-in AskUserQuestion tool, show a VS Code QuickPick per question and feed the user's selections back to the model. When false (default), the questions are answered with the fallback template (interactiveQuestionsTemplateId), telling the agent to proceed autonomously. Requires useBuiltInTools; has no effect when toolApprovalMode is 'never' (canUseTool is not fired under bypassPermissions).

interactiveQuestionsTemplateId.description: Selected fallback template id (from anthropic.interactiveQuestionsTemplates) returned to the agent when interactive questions are disabled or the user dismisses the picker. Empty = built-in default.

anthropicMemory.description: Cross-config memory defaults applied when a configuration does not override them.

targetFile.description: File name within the chosen scope (e.g. facts.md).
